Key catalysts ahead include ECB's non-monetary meeting on May 20, June monetary policy decision, and U.S. CPI/nonfarm payrolls releases, which could test support at 1.16 or push toward 1.20 resistance per 2026 forecasts averaging 1.18–1.22.

markets.question: Will Orinats Yerkir win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

Upsets could stem from late scandals, opposition mergers, low turnout, or Azerbaijan border escalation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 86.5% implied probability of no change in the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's Official Cash Rate (OCR) at its May 27 Monetary Policy Statement, reflecting sticky annual CPI inflation at 3.1% for the March 2026 quarter—unchanged from prior periods and above the 1-3% target midpoint—balanced against a softening labor market with unemployment easing to 5.3% in Q1 2026 from 5.4%. The Monetary Policy Committee held the OCR steady at 2.25% in April amid oil-driven price pressures, while the May Financial Stability Report flagged global uncertainties from Middle East tensions slowing New Zealand's economic recovery. A 14% hike chance stems from persistent inflation risks, with cuts near zero given weak growth signals; traders eye the MPS for forward guidance shifts.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas" reclaimed the Billboard Hot 100 summit for the May 9 chart, marking her fifth stint at #1 this year and highlighting sustained country crossover appeal amid streaming surges and radio play. Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at #1 the prior week, propelled by massive presales and viral TikTok traction, while Bruno Mars holds #2 with "I Just Might" buoyed by enduring collabs. Eight artists have notched #1s so far—Mariah Carey, Taylor Swift (twice), Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, Ella Langley (dominant), and Olivia Rodrigo—leaving room for pop heavyweights like Ariana Grande or Drake via summer album singles. Traders eye weekly chart freezes Tuesdays and precursor metrics like Spotify streams for late-year frontrunners.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Western Conference semifinals series between the San Antonio Spurs and Minnesota Timberwolves stands tied 2-2 heading into Game 5 at Frost Bank Center, where the Spurs hold home-court advantage from their dominant 62-20 regular-season mark as the No. 2 West seed. Timberwolves stole momentum in Game 4 with a 114-109 rally, fueled by Anthony Edwards' 36 points—including 16 in the fourth—and Victor Wembanyama's early second-quarter ejection for a flagrant-2 elbow on Naz Reid, allowing Rudy Gobert and Jaden McDaniels to feast inside. Spurs guard De'Aaron Fox is questionable with left ankle soreness from the loss, clouding their backcourt alongside Cade Cunningham, while Minnesota's core remains healthy barring minor role-player absences. Home rest and Wembanyama's redemption bid loom large for trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $120 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-25 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-25 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Joseph Morelle's commanding trader consensus at 93.5% stems from NY-25's D+10 partisan lean, his consistent general election victories—including 61% in 2024—and dominant fundraising edge ahead of the June 23 closed primaries, where challengers Cassandra Lin Leach, Sherita Traywick, and Robin Wilt trail far behind. markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Minas Gerais gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo (Republicanos), a gospel singer-turned-senator with strong evangelical backing, leads trader consensus at implied 56.5% probability of victory in the Minas Gerais gubernatorial race, driven by dominant showings in recent polls like Genial/Quaest (April 22-26, 2026) at 30-37% first-round and decisive runoff wins, and Doxa (May 1-3) at 28-30% ahead of Alexandre Kalil (PDT). Incumbent Governor Mateus Simões (PSD), elevated after Romeu Zema's March resignation, polls weakly at 7%, while Senate President Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB) at 18.5% benefits from center-right momentum despite lower polling (6-12%). Kalil's 10.7% reflects runoff viability (34-38% vs. Cleitinho), with the October 4 first round and potential October 25 runoff defining outcomes amid stable multi-candidate fields.

markets.question: Will MrBeast say "Feastables" during his next YouTube video?
markets.description:
MrBeast releases videos on https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mr Beast says the listed term during the next video he releases on YouTube. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

If clips of old videos or prerecorded clips are aired where people are speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will count toward this market's resolution.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

If no such video is posted by MrBeast by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be the released video from (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast).

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Cook Political Report rates the race Solid Democratic, citing Jeffries' reelection strength despite past activist pressures; a potential primary challenger, NYC Councilmember Chi Ossé, ended his bid in December 2025, leaving Jeffries unopposed ahead of the June 23 closed primary. No prominent Republican nominee has emerged post-April filing deadline, reinforcing historical blowout margins. Upsets would require a major Jeffries scandal, unexpectedly weak Democratic nominee, or national Republican midterm wave overwhelming district demographics.

markets.question: Will Algeria vs. Austria end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for June 27, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Austria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, June 27, 2026 between Algeria and Austria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Algeria's mounting goalkeeper crisis, with Anthony Mandrea sidelined by shoulder surgery, Melvin Mastil out from hernia issues, and Luca Zidane doubtful after a jaw fracture, has eroded their defensive edge ahead of this pivotal World Cup Group J clash against Austria on June 27 in Kansas City. Both teams enter with strong recent form—Algeria unbeaten in qualifiers with solid home/away records, Austria ranked slightly higher at No. 24 versus Algeria's 28—fueling trader consensus for a razor-thin contest on neutral ground at Arrowhead Stadium. Key absences like Ramy Bensebaini's ankle injury for the Fennecs balance Austria's own concerns over Maximilian Wöber's muscular problems, while both sides eye knockout progression behind Argentina and ahead of Jordan, keeping win probabilities tightly clustered around 37% for Austria and 36% for Algeria.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lucknow be 47°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chaudhary Charan Singh Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/in/lucknow/VILK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will SpaceX’s initial public offering occur in September 2026 (ET)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on the calendar month (ET) in 2026 in which SpaceX’s initial public offering (IPO) occurs.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2027".

Announcements, filings, or scheduling of an IPO will not suffice; this market will resolve after public trading has begun.

Resolution will be based on information from the primary exchange.

events.title: In which month will SpaceX IPO?
events.description:
This market will resolve based on the calendar month (ET) in 2026 in which SpaceX’s initial public offering (IPO) occurs.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2027".

Announcements, filings, or scheduling of an IPO will not suffice; this market will resolve after public trading has begun.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 68.5% implied probability for a SpaceX initial public offering in June, driven by recent leaks detailing a confidential S-1 filing with the SEC in early April, accelerated employee stock vesting last week, and an imminent public prospectus expected the week of May 18. Banker meetings confirmed a roadshow starting June 8 and pricing the week of June 15 at a targeted $1.75–2 trillion valuation with a record $75 billion raise, up to 30% allocated to retail investors. Spillover to August (19.6%) and July (9.4%) reflects potential SEC review delays or market volatility, while "No IPO before 2027" at 5.4% underscores strong momentum absent major setbacks. Key catalysts include the public S-1 release and Starship launch cadence updates.

markets.question: Exact Score: Kilmarnock FC 1 - 1 Dundee FC?
markets.description: In the upcoming Scottish Premiership game between Kilmarnock FC and Dundee FC, scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 2:45 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the Kilmarnock FC vs. Dundee FC match originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 2:45 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

If the actual score is not one of the explicitly listed outcomes, the market resolves to "Any Other Score." eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the razor-thin margins in this Scottish Premiership relegation scrap at Rugby Park, with Kilmarnock hosting Dundee FC in a pivotal penultimate fixture. Both sides sit precariously in the lower table—Kilmarnock around 10th, Dundee just above—after grinding out a tense 1-1 draw in their April head-to-head marred by late VAR drama. Kilmarnock's recent 3-0 home win over Dundee United provides momentum and a clean sheet boost, but long-term absentees like Tyreece John-Jules, Djenairo Daniels, and Matty Kennedy thin their attack. Dundee grapples with goalkeeper Jon McCracken's season-ending ankle injury, exposing defensive vulnerabilities. Historical H2H tilts toward low-scoring stalemates (average 2.77 goals), home/away form parity, and mutual struggles for clean sheets keep exact score probabilities tightly bunched across 0-0, 1-0, 1-1, and other narrow outcomes.

markets.question: Will Hudson Williams be named People's Sexiest Man Alive in 2026?

markets.question: Will Wesley Said be the top goal scorer in the 2025-26 Ligue 1 season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Only goals scored in Ligue 1 matches will count. Goals scored in other competitions (e.g., Coupe de France, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Ligue 1 season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Ligue 1. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

With just 2-3 matchdays left in the 2025-26 season, his lead reflects Rennes' push up the table and his 5 assists underscoring sustained threat. Challengers would need extraordinary hauls—like multiple hat-tricks—coupled with Lepaul injury absence or blank sheets in remaining fixtures to overtake, scenarios traders deem remote given his fitness and matchup edges.

markets.question: Variational FDV above $4B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Variational's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Variational doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will MrBeast's next video get less than 50 million views on week 1?

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Minor candidates like Charles Herbster—a 2022 primary loser—Sal Holguin, Sheila Korth-Focken, Gary L. eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's trader consensus at 53% implied probability for the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon title stems from his unchallenged world No. 1 ranking and blistering clay-court dominance, including a straight-sets Madrid Open final rout of Alexander Zverev for his fourth straight title and ongoing Italian Open progress amid a 25-match win streak. Americans like Ben Shelton (2.4%) and Taylor Fritz (2.3%) draw support from grass-court prowess, but lack Sinner's current momentum as the grass swing approaches post-RG.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 94% for Les Wexner facing criminal charges by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or active federal prosecution despite recent Epstein-related scrutiny. Wexner, named a potential co-conspirator in 2019 FBI documents, underwent a February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition where he denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes and claimed to have been duped, with no subsequent charges filed. Civil lawsuits by Epstein victims in March-April 2026 allege financial enablement but carry no criminal implications. Congressional inquiries, including questions to Attorney General Pam Bondi, have yielded no prosecutorial action, underscoring high barriers to timely indictment absent new evidence or grand jury developments.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include National + Labour?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Ukraine coup attempt by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in Ukraine at any point between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Ukrainian government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Ukrainian government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certain stability in Ukraine's government through June 30, with "No" at 96%, driven by the absence of any credible reports or intelligence on coup plotting amid ongoing martial law imposed since Russia's 2022 invasion. This framework bans elections, protests, and political opposition, channeling military loyalty to the eastern front against Russian advances rather than internal challenges. January 2026 cabinet reshuffles by President Zelenskyy neutralized potential rivals without sparking unrest, while recent mobilization tensions have produced localized protests but no organized threats from security apparatus or elites. Battlefield stalemates persist without major defeats that could trigger mutiny; shifts could arise from abrupt Western aid cuts, elite defections, or sudden territorial losses.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: HOTU vs INOX Division (BO3) - CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round of 16 match between HOTU and INOX Division in the CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 12 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "HOTU" if HOTU win the match against INOX Division.

This market will resolve to "INOX Division" if INOX Division win the match against HOTU.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: HOTU enters the CCT Europe Series 1 playoffs Round of 16 as the higher-ranked squad at #33, holding a direct invite after mixed recent results including group stage exits at IEM Rio and PGL Astana qualifiers, bolstered by kade0's arrival on April 30. INOX Division, ranked #64 with an 82.6% win rate over the past three months, advanced from the Swiss group stage via a 3-2 record, highlighted by upsets over TDK, Omega, and MOUZ NXT in the last week, despite a new roster assembled April 20 from eLITenergy. No head-to-head history exists in this BO3, with maps Nuke (INOX 64% win rate), Dust2 (HOTU 62%), and Ancient (both ~67%) suggesting a competitive trader consensus on a tight matchup influenced by form momentum and stylistic edges.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket semifinal 2 match between paiN and FUT Esports in the IEM Atlanta Group A, initially scheduled for May 12 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if paiN and FUT Esports play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both teams enter the IEM Atlanta 2026 Group A upper bracket semi-final on strong momentum after decisive openers yesterday: paiN dominated FaZe 2-0 on Dust2 (13-6) and Anubis (13-8), fueled by snow's 1.47 rating and piriajr's rifling prowess in saffee's first LAN since rejoining April 22; FUT Esports dispatched NRG 2-0 via close Ancient (16-13) and Overpass (13-9) triumphs. FUT holds edges in world ranking (#10 vs. paiN #32), Valve beta (#6 vs. #17), and past-three-month stats (1.13 rating, 74.5% KAST), with Krabeni's tactical IGLing key amid a PGL Bucharest title run. No head-to-head exists; map vetoes like Inferno (paiN 87% winrate) or Mirage (FUT 63%) loom large in this double-elim BO3, where top-three advance to playoffs.

markets.question: Will Z.ai have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Albania come in last place at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 places last in the final.

If no last place country is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions the United Kingdom as the frontrunner for last place in the Eurovision 2026 Grand Final at 36.5% implied probability, driven by polarizing reactions to Look Mum No Computer's experimental electronic entry "Eins, Zwei, Drei" following first rehearsal clips released this week, which many fans labeled "weird," "grating," and a potential "nul points" repeat amid the UK's history of televote struggles. Austria trails at 26.5%, reflecting underwhelming buzz for host entry "Tanzschein" by Cosmó despite its national selection win via Vienna Calling, with bookmakers echoing concerns over staging and broad appeal as rehearsals highlight execution risks. Germany's 9% stems from solid Das deutsche Finale contenders but competitive second semi-final dynamics; all Big Five and host auto-qualify, so final performances and jury-televote splits remain pivotal ahead of May 16.

markets.question: Will Lovable be acquired before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if credible reporting confirms that any entity enters into an agreement to acquire the listed company by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Mergers where the listed company is subsumed by another entity will count toward a "Yes" resolution.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed company and/or its leadership; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the tech-focused acquisition market hinges on SpaceX's April 2026 option to buy AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion, a pivotal developer tool in the race for superior large language model integrations, catapulting its position amid Elon Musk's xAI ambitions and Microsoft's prior interest. Biotech standout Viking Therapeutics draws bids in the booming obesity drug sector with GLP-1 candidates like VK2735 nearing key trials, while gaming firm Ubisoft faces persistent rumors amid slumping shares and live-service pivots. Caesars Entertainment buzz stems from Tilman Fertitta and Carl Icahn takeover talks. Watch Q2 earnings, FDA milestones, and Cursor's option deadline for swings, as AI M&A surges with regulatory scrutiny on Big Tech consolidations.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 7°C or below on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Météo-France's latest forecasts point to a high of 13–14°C for Paris on May 14, 2026, amid overcast skies, persistent showers (90% probability, ~6 mm accumulation), and gusty winds up to 48 km/h from the north, reflecting a cool, unstable weather pattern gripping Île-de-France this week. This marks the coldest day in the May 11–17 outlook, with highs struggling below seasonal norms of ~18°C due to a lingering low-pressure system and cold air advection. Recent observations confirm the chilly trend, following cooler-than-average early May days after brief warmer spikes. Model consensus holds firm, though minor shifts possible with tomorrow's updates; traders weigh inherent short-term forecast uncertainty against real-time observations from official stations.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

events.title: Next Brazil Senate Election: Most Seats Won
events.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Brazilian Senate, not by any federation or coalition it may be a part of.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to win the most seats in Brazil's 2026 Senate election, where 54 seats (two per state) will be contested on October 4 amid a fragmented opposition landscape. Recent state-level polls, including Genial/Quaest in Paraná (early May 2026) showing right-wing pre-candidates dominating, and VEJA's March consolidation across 17 states highlighting PL leads in Santa Catarina (Carlos and Carol de Toni Bolsonaro) and Distrito Federal (Michelle Bolsonaro), have solidified this positioning. PL entered 2026 as the Senate's largest caucus (15 seats) and boasts the most competitive candidacies per CNN and Estadão analyses (12 potential wins), outpacing MDB's strengths in the Northeast. Government-aligned PT trails amid low polling, with no major shifts in the past week.

markets.question: Will any OpenAI GPT model score at least 70% on the FrontierMath Exam?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any OpenAI GPT model achieves the listed score or greater on the FrontierMath Exam by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve according to the Epoch AI’s Frontier Math benchmarking leaderboard (https://epoch.ai/frontiermath) for Tier 1-3. Studies which are not included in the leaderboard (e.g. https://x.com/EpochAIResearch/status/1945905796904005720) will not be considered.

The primary resolution source will be information from EpochAI; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: OpenAI GPT score on FrontierMath Benchmark by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any OpenAI GPT model achieves the listed score or greater on the FrontierMath Exam by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve according to the Epoch AI’s Frontier Math benchmarking leaderboard (https://epoch.ai/frontiermath) for Tier 1-3. Studies which are not included in the leaderboard (e.g. https://x.com/EpochAIResearch/status/1945905796904005720) will not be considered.

The primary resolution source will be information from EpochAI; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro currently leads the FrontierMath Tiers 1-3 benchmark at 50%, falling short of the 60% threshold for market resolution, but trader sentiment hinges on rapid iteration in reasoning models and an Epoch AI review—initiated May 11, 2026—that flagged fatal errors in about one-third of Tiers 1-4 problems using GPT-5.5 itself, potentially inflating true capabilities via corrected scores. GPT-5.5, released April 23, advanced Tier 4 to 39.6% while dominating agentic benchmarks like Terminal-Bench at 82.7%, underscoring OpenAI's math-focused scaling. Competitive pressure mounts from DeepMind's recent 47.9% Tier 4 record, with upcoming GPT-5.6 previews or early GPT-6 tests eyed before June 30 amid benchmark fixes.

markets.question: Will Arsenal finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's five-point lead atop the Premier League table with a superior +42 goal difference after 36 games—bolstered by a gritty 1-0 win over West Ham via Leandro Trossard's late strike in matchweek 36—has solidified Manchester City's position as trader consensus (86%) to finish second, with the Citizens holding a game in hand but trailing at 74 points from 35 matches following their 3-0 rout of Brentford. Man United lag 11 points back, rendering other challengers negligible. City's upcoming home clash with Crystal Palace tomorrow precedes the FA Cup final against Chelsea, potentially disrupting rhythm amid tougher remaining fixtures versus Bournemouth away and Aston Villa, while Arsenal host promoted Burnley before traveling to Palace. Arsenal slips to second only if they drop points in both finals and City sweeps, leveraging head-to-head edge and goals-scored tiebreaker over goal difference.

markets.question: Will Israel win the first Eurovision Semi-Final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" for the listed country whose candidate wins the most points at the Eurovision First Semi-Final, scheduled for May 12, 2026.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win the most points at the Eurovision 2026 First Semi-Final based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If this competition is not held or no ranking is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of the First Semi-Final for Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by violin virtuoso Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen commands 62.5% market-implied odds as the clear frontrunner to win Eurovision 2026's First Semi-Final, propelled by explosive dress rehearsals showcasing cinematic staging and flawless live vocals that topped jury assessments and ranked second in press polls. Greece's Akylas follows at 15.5% after "Ferto" led those same polls with its viral hook and dynamic stage presence in recent jury shows. Israel's Noam Bettan holds 13.5% on strong diaspora televote expectations for "Michelle," despite an EBU warning to broadcaster KAN over voting ads amid boycott tensions. Sweden lingers at 3.1% buoyed by early running order and jury favoritism. With tonight's live show at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle determining the ten Grand Final qualifiers, last-minute audience reactions could spark shifts in this tepid semi-final field.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado's 1st Congressional District, a longtime Democratic stronghold centered in urban Denver with a strong partisan voting index favoring Democrats by around 25-30 points, underpins trader consensus pricing the Democratic Party nominee at 92.5% to win the November 3, 2026, general election. Incumbent Rep. Diana DeGette advanced to the June 30 Democratic primary despite trailing challenger Melat Kiros in the March district assembly vote, where she qualified via petition amid anti-incumbent sentiment; Wanda James also qualified, but DeGette's fundraising edge and experience position her as the likely nominee. No prominent Republican primary challengers have emerged, reinforcing the lopsided odds. Upsets could stem from a weakened Democratic nominee post-primary, a major scandal, health issues, or an overwhelming national Republican wave, though historical precedents in safe seats make these low-probability scenarios.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Jeddah be 40°C or higher on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the King Abdulaziz International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sa/jeddah/OEJN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecast ensembles from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and Global Forecast System (GFS) project Jeddah's highest temperature on May 14 at 37–38°C, fueling trader consensus with 33% implied probability for 38°C and 26% for 37°C amid model spread of about 1°C. A persistent upper-level ridge over the Arabian Peninsula drives subsidence and clear skies, boosting daytime heating, while Red Sea sea breezes introduce variability by moderating coastal peaks through enhanced low-level moisture and convergence. The National Center for Meteorology (NCM) indicates above-average May temperatures regionwide, consistent with recent highs near 40°C inland but tempered in Jeddah. New 00Z model runs and NCM advisories expected overnight could refine exact outcomes as resolution nears.

markets.question: Will Independiente Santa Fe vs. América de Cali end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 12, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Independiente Santa Fe's home advantage at high-altitude Estadio El Campín faces América de Cali's resilient away form in this tightly contested Liga BetPlay Apertura matchup, driving trader consensus to near-even implied probabilities across win, draw, and América victory. Santa Fe's unbeaten run in their last five league games (one win, four draws) signals defensive solidity but limited firepower, compounded by recent injury concerns including Víctor Moreno's questionable knee status, Ewil Murillo's tendinopathy, and recoveries for Yilmar Velásquez and Maximiliano Lovera as of May 2 updates. América grapples with Yeison Guzmán's likely absence and lingering issues for Jhon Murillo and Marcos Mina, while their 1-0 February win highlights mutual tactical discipline in head-to-heads, keeping the race unpredictably close.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Zagreb: Yannick Alexandrescou vs Thanasi Kokkinakis
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Yannick Alexandrescou and Thanasi Kokkinakis in the Zagreb, originally scheduled for May 12, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Yannick Alexandrescou' if Yannick Alexandrescou advances against Thanasi Kokkinakis.

This market will resolve to 'Thanasi Kokkinakis' if Thanasi Kokkinakis advances against Yannick Alexandrescou.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Thanasi Kokkinakis, returning from a four-month injury layoff at ATP No. 892, advanced to the Zagreb Open Challenger main draw via wildcard qualifying with straight-sets wins over No. 294 Daniel Michalski (7-6(3), 6-3) and No. 282 Michele Ribecai (6-7(3), 6-3, 6-0) on outdoor clay over the past 48 hours, signaling match sharpness despite limited play since January. He faces Yannick Theodor Alexandrescou, an 18-year-old French junior world No. 3 granted direct entry through the ATP Junior Accelerator program following a recent three-set Challenger debut loss in Francavilla. With no head-to-head, trader consensus reflects Kokkinakis' veteran baseline power and clay adaptability against Alexandrescou's promising but unproven pro-level experience, amid first-round implications for ranking points on a slower surface favoring endurance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Genial/Quaest polling from April 23-27 shows a technical tie in Bahia's gubernatorial race, with challenger ACM Neto (União Brasil) at 41% edging incumbent Jerônimo Rodrigues (PT) at 37-38% in first- and second-round scenarios within a 3-point margin, driving trader consensus to a slim 52-48 lead for the governor. This closely contested rematch from 2022 reflects PT's entrenched rural and machine support against opposition unity and Neto's Salvador popularity, amid ongoing critiques of Jerônimo's infrastructure delays—like unfinished works spotlighted this week. Jerônimo's higher rejection rate offsets incumbency advantages, keeping dynamics tight; fresh polls, debates, or scandal developments like the Banco Master probe before October's first round could create separation.

Resolutions on signatures, upcoming debates, and fundraising reports could separate the pack, as incumbency-free dynamics and MAGA faction support keep odds volatile.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which banks will fail by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework, within the listed date range:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open until September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices bank failures by June 30 at low implied probabilities—BNY Mellon leading at around 9%, KeyBank at 3%, and others below 5%—reflecting sector stability after just two small U.S. failures year-to-date: Metropolitan Capital Bank in January and Community Bank and Trust later. FDIC's Q4 2025 data shows problem banks at a normal 1.4% of institutions with steady return on assets, despite commercial real estate (CRE) pressures from $875 billion in maturing debt and high office vacancies stressing regional lenders. Easing lending standards and robust Deposit Insurance Fund provide buffers, though Q2 earnings and CRE workouts could shift sentiment ahead of the deadline.

markets.question: Will Prosperous Armenia win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Civil Contract's commanding 92.5% implied probability on Polymarket reflects recent polling dominance ahead of Armenia's June 7 parliamentary elections under proportional representation, where the incumbent party led by Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan consistently secures pluralities despite fragmented opposition. The latest EVN Report survey (April 1–May 2, 2026) shows Civil Contract at 32.5% among decided voters—well ahead of Strong Armenia (10.1%) and others below electoral thresholds—with 39.5% undecided leaning incumbent via rising approval ratings (49% for Pashinyan) and improved perceptions of economy and security. Opposition alliances like Armenia Alliance (4.4%) fail to consolidate, projecting Civil Contract 40–51% vote share. Upsets could stem from late scandals, opposition mergers, low turnout, or Azerbaijan border escalation.

markets.question: New York Red Bulls vs. Columbus Crew: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: New York Red Bulls vs. Columbus Crew - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: New York Red Bulls hold seventh place in the MLS Eastern Conference standings with 11 points from seven matches (3-2-2, GD -4), buoyed by solid home form including three wins in their last six at Red Bull Arena, while Columbus Crew sit 11th on six points (1-3-3, GD -1) amid road woes with three losses in six away games. Recent Matchday 7 results saw Crew draw goalless with Chicago and lose 0-1 to Nashville earlier, contrasting Red Bulls' mixed bag including a home win over FC Cincinnati. Key absences shape lineups: Red Bulls without Justin Che (hamstring, late April return) and AJ Marcucci (knee), Cameron Harper questionable (hamstring); Crew missing Mohamed Farsi (sports hernia). Even head-to-head record (NYRB 28W-30L-15D) points to a tight Eastern Conference battle on May 13.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk and Sam Altman settle?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman in Musk v. Altman et al., Case No. 4:24-cv-04722-YGR (U.S. District Court, Northern District of California), are settled by 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A settlement refers to an agreement between Elon Musk and Sam Altman that resolves all claims between them in this case, whether through a formal settlement agreement, stipulated dismissal, or other court-recognized resolution indicating that the claims between these two parties have been resolved. Announcements of such a settlement will qualify, regardless of whether final paperwork is completed or court approval occurs after the deadline.

Settlements involving other parties (e.g., OpenAI or any additional defendants) will not count unless they also resolve all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman.

If all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman are resolved through a final court judgment (e.g., trial verdict or dispositive ruling), or through a unilateral dismissal of claims not constituting a settlement, before any qualifying settlement occurs, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. court system (including filings, orders, or docket updates). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. markets.question: Cordoba: Eduardo Ribeiro vs Murkel Dellien
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Eduardo Ribeiro and Murkel Dellien in the Cordoba, originally scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Eduardo Ribeiro' if Eduardo Ribeiro advances against Murkel Dellien.

This market will resolve to 'Murkel Dellien' if Murkel Dellien advances against Eduardo Ribeiro.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
