markets.question: Will Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) hit (LOW) $80 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.NFLX%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 94.1% implied probability that the UK will not designate Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation by June 30, driven by the Labour government's procedural delays despite mounting pressure. In late April 2026, Prime Minister Keir Starmer confirmed plans for new legislation to empower ministers to proscribe state-backed groups like the IRGC—following the EU's January designation—but no bill has been tabled in Parliament as of mid-May. Cross-party MPs and the London Assembly continue urging immediate action under the Terrorism Act 2000, yet Home Secretary Yvette Cooper has not issued a proscription order, with upcoming parliamentary sessions lacking confirmed votes amid historical caution over diplomatic fallout. This timeline leaves scant room for resolution before the deadline.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

markets.question: Will there be at least 1900 measles cases in the U.S. by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: As of May 7, 2026, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) reports 1,842 confirmed measles cases in the United States for the year, with 1,830 among U.S. residents across 39 jurisdictions and 12 international visitors; 93% are outbreak-associated from 25 new 2026 outbreaks, led by South Carolina (669 cases), Utah (439), Texas (182), and Florida (135). This surge stems from importation-linked transmission in under-vaccinated communities amid global measles upticks, though recent weekly data shows only seven new cases for the week of May 3, signaling a slowdown from earlier peaks. Traders should watch state surveillance reports feeding into CDC's weekly updates, with final tallies through May 31 determining resolution amid potential reporting lags or late surges.

markets.question: Will Lucid announce bankruptcy before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company announces that it will file for bankruptcy or has filed for bankruptcy of any variety by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement will suffice for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether or not the actual filing occurs.

The announcement must be made through any of their official or verified channels, including a recorded or written statement by their CEO, legal representation, or any other individual or team that officially represents the company.

A definitive consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?
markets.description:
This polymarket will resolve to "Yes" if Cristiano Ronaldo officially announces his retirement from club soccer by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To resolve "Yes", the announcement must clearly state that Ronaldo is retiring from club-level professional soccer. The announcement must come from Ronaldo himself or his official representatives.

Retirement from international soccer alone will not qualify. 

The retirement must be intended to take effect immediately or prior to the start of the next club season following the announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Cristiano Ronaldo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cristiano Ronaldo's sustained elite form in the Saudi Pro League, where he reached 100 goals for Al-Nassr by early May 2026 and notched recent strikes like his header against Al-Ahli on April 29, underpins trader consensus pricing "No" at 88.5% implied probability for a 2026 retirement announcement. His June 2025 contract extension through 2027, combined with Portugal coach Roberto Martínez's March statement affirming no retirement plans, signals commitment amid a title-chasing campaign and preparations for the 2026 World Cup—widely viewed as his international swan song. Absent injury concerns or sudden withdrawals, these factors position Ronaldo to extend his club career beyond 2026, aligning with his pursuit of 1,000 career goals.

markets.question: Will Finland win on 2026-05-31?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 31, 2026
If Finland wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Germany vs. Finland
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Sunday, May 31, 2026 between Germany and Finland.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters the May 31 international friendly in Mainz as overwhelming trader favorites at 85% implied probability, driven by their superior FIFA ranking, home advantage at MEWA Arena, and recent dominant form including Florian Wirtz's masterclass—two goals and two assists—in a thrilling victory over Switzerland six weeks ago. Head-to-head history favors Die Mannschaft, unbeaten against Finland with a 2-0 win in their last 2016 friendly, underscoring the quality gap. Despite Serge Gnabry's long-term muscle tear ruling him out for the 2025-26 season and recovery concerns for Jamal Musiala and Kai Havertz, Germany's squad depth maintains edge. Finland, hampered by Lucas Bergström's injury and lacking firepower, sees draw and win at 10% each, reflecting limited upset potential absent major German absences.

markets.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 PGA Championship tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 23, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

events.description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed player finishes in the top 20 at the 2026 PGA Championship tournament, including ties. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If final results are not announced by May 23, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "No". The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 PGA Championship tees off Thursday at Aronimink Golf Club, a Donald Ross par-70 with 174 bunkers favoring precision ball-strikers over bombers, in a 156-player field finalized this week including LIV Golf stars like Jon Rahm and Bryson DeChambeau. Defending champion Scottie Scheffler headlines alongside Masters winner Rory McIlroy and FedEx Cup leader Cameron Young, whose two PGA Tour victories this season have elevated his contender status. Tee times feature high-profile groups, such as McIlroy with Jordan Spieth and Rahm, while Phil Mickelson's recent withdrawal for health reasons opens spots; no major injuries plague top-ranked players. Course history is sparse since 1962, emphasizing recent form, stroke play consistency, and cut-line survival through Sunday amid mild Pennsylvania weather.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors SpaceX at 86.5% implied probability for the largest 2026 IPO by market cap, driven by its early April confidential filing targeting a June listing with a potential $1.75 trillion valuation and $75 billion raise—the largest ever. This follows February's all-stock merger with xAI, valued at $250 billion, integrating AI capabilities with Starlink's satellite internet dominance (projected $20 billion 2026 revenue) and orbital data center ambitions, outpacing rivals reliant on rented cloud infrastructure. xAI lingers at 25.5% amid merger overhang, while Anthropic's 9.5% reflects groundwork for an October IPO at up to $630 billion; OpenAI trails at 2.9% due to CFO signaling unreadiness despite massive private funding. Watch SpaceX roadshow and retail investor events this month for resolution catalysts.

markets.question: Will Mayumi Tanaka as Monkey D. Luffy (ONE PIECE) win Best Anime Voice Artist Performance (Japanese) at the 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards?
markets.description:
The 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards are scheduled to occur in Japan on May 23, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed individual who wins the award for Best Anime Voice Artist Performance (Japanese) at the 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed individual whose name comes first in alphabetical order.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Daiki Yamashita's portrayal of Izuku Midoriya in My Hero Academia's final season a slim 25.5% implied probability edge in the Crunchyroll Anime Awards Best Anime Voice Artist Performance (Japanese) category, fueled by the series' 15 nominations and emotional climax drawing massive fan mobilization during the April 3-14 voting window. Close behind are Chainsaw Man – The Movie: Reze Arc's Kikunosuke Toya as Denji (21.8%) and Reina Ueda as Reze (19.2%), buoyed by the film's 13 total nods and hype around its explosive action sequences. Mayumi Tanaka's iconic Monkey D. Luffy from One Piece (21.5%) leverages decades of legacy appeal, while Aoi Yuki's nuanced Maomao in The Apothecary Diaries Season 2 (16.5%) benefits from critical acclaim. With voting closed, the May 23 Tokyo ceremony looms as jury votes blend fan sentiment and expert picks in this fanbase-driven showdown.

markets.question: Hurupay FDV above $30M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Hurupay's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Hurupay (https://x.com/HurupayApp) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 23°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's 9-day weather forecast, updated at 16:30 HKT on May 12, projects a 26–32°C temperature range for May 13 under a southerly airstream delivering warm, moist air, sunny periods, and light south-to-southwest winds of force 2–3, fostering an overnight minimum of 26°C amid 60–90% relative humidity and isolated showers. This aligns with recent observations of soil temperatures at 26.3°C and daytime highs near 30–32°C, reflecting above-normal May conditions in a normal-to-above-normal seasonal outlook. Trader consensus at 96% implied probability for 26°C reflects model agreement on minimal nocturnal cooling from the hot daytime setup and urban heat island effects. Realistic challenges include unexpected heavy showers or a shift to stronger winds introducing cooler air, with the next forecast update at 11:30 HKT on May 13 potentially refining overnight guidance ahead of official Observatory measurements.
